Description:en: Type of leukocyte, with coarse granules that stain blue when exposed to a basic dye. Basophils normally constitute 1% or less of the total white blood cell count but may increase or decrease in certain diseases.  This is a microscopic exam.
Myocardial band fraction of creatine kinase in percent
Measurement of creatinine in 24 hour urinary sample in mmol/L
Urine creatinine clearance, or the volume of plasma cleared of creatinine per unit of time, taken through 24-hour collection
Bacterial culture of CSF sample

Fully specified name:fr: Test de coloration de Gram du LCR
Description:en: Microscopic Gram stain of cerebrospinal fluid (CSF). Mapped to LOINC 14357-8 (Gram stain in CSF). Used as a rapid preliminary step to describe bacterial morphology and Gram reaction when evaluating suspected meningitis or ventriculitis; results are almost always qualitative or narrative (e.g. Gram-positive cocci in pairs; Gram-negative bacilli; polymicrobial pattern; no organisms seen). No numeric absolute low or high; critical low or high; or normal range applies because the datatype is Text rather than a quantitative scale.
Description:fr: Coloration de Gram microscopique du liquide céphalorachidien (LCR). LOINC 14357-8. Examen rapide pour décrire la morphologie bactérienne et la réaction de Gram (méningite ou ventriculite suspectée). Résultat le plus souvent qualitatif ou narratif. Pas de valeurs numériques de référence (basse ou haute absolue; critiques; normales) car le type de donnée est Texte.

Fully specified name:fr: Mesure de protéines dans le liquide céphalo-rachidien
Description:en: Examination of protein level in CSF in mg/dL

Fully specified name:en: HIV DNA polymerase chain reaction, dried blood spot (DBS)
Fully specified name:fr: Réaction en chaîne par polymérase de l'ADN du VIH, goutte de sang séché (DBS)

Description:en: A type of white blood cell found in vertebrate blood, containing cytoplasmic granules that are easily stained by eosin or other acid dyes.  This is a microscopic exam.
The rate of fluid filtration from the glomerular capillaries to the Bowman's capsule, derived with the help of nuclear scan
Glycosylated hemoglobin A (HbA1c) test is measured in percent and is gives an indication of average serum glucose levels over several months.
Qualitative interpretation of MTB stimulated gamma interferon from whole blood.

Fully specified name:en: Line probe assay for tuberculosis DST
Description:en: Line probe assays are tests that use PCR and reverse hybridization methods for the rapid detection of mutations associated with drug resistance. Line probe assays are designed to identify M. tuberculosis complex and simultaneously detect mutations associated with drug resistance. - See more at: http://www.tbfacts.org/drug-susceptibility/#sthash.5AeY4QuX.dpuf
Lab test to measure the amount of lithium in the blood.
Agranulocytic leukocyte that normally makes up about 25% of the total white blood cell count but increases in the presence of infection.  This is a microscopic exam.

Fully specified name:en: Combined % of monocytes, eosinophils and basophils
Fully specified name:fr: Mélangés en pourcentage (monocytes, des éosinophiles et des basophiles), les cellules sanguines
Description:en: A lab test obtained by summing the monocytes, eosinophils and basophils.

Large, circulating, phagocytic white blood cells, having a single well-defined nucleus and very fine granulation in the cytoplasm. Monocytes constitute from 3 to 8 percent of the white blood cells in humans.  This is a microscopic exam.

Absolute neutrophil count (ANC) is a measure of the number of neutrophil granulocytes (also known as polymorphonuclear cells, PMN's, polys, granulocytes, segmented neutrophils or segs) present in the blood. Neutrophils are a type of white blood cell that fights against infection. ANC = (%neutrophils + %bands) x WBC
Test to find, count and examine Neutrophils the type of white blood cell which form an early line of defence against bacterial infections by using microscopic exam

Fully specified name:fr: Panel glucose test de tolérance, 5 Specimens (75g)
Description:en: A series of tests to measure blood glucose over a 4-hour period of time after drinking a 75 gram bottle of glucose solution.

Fully specified name:en: N-terminal pro brain natriuretic peptide (NT-proBNP) measurement
Slit skin smear for acid fast bacilli
Wet mount of skin snip for Onchocerca

Fully specified name:fr: Modèle de la culture pour la tuberculose
Description:en: Contains concepts related to tuberculosis culture.
A throat culture is a laboratory diagnostic test to isolate and identify organisms that may cause infection in the throat. Sampling is performed by throat swab
Total protein measured in plasma or serum
Patient's temperature in degrees centigrade.
Lab test that is performed prior to a blood transfusion in order to determine if the donor's blood is compatible with the blood of an intended recipient.
Lab test that measures the body's ability to use glucose. This is the first of 5 samples, prior to drinking the bottle of glucose solution.
Test for bacteria in urine. Can be done by dipstick or microscopic methods.
Immunology Test - CD45 plus lymphocyte count.

A monocyte count is part of a complete blood count and is expressed by absolute numbers.  Increase a monocyte count include: - chronic inflammation - stress response - hyperadrenocorticism - immune mediated disease - pyogranulomatous disease - necrosis - red cell regeneration.
Basophils are the least common of the granulocytes, representing about 0.5% to 1% of circulating leukocytes.
Percentage of nucleated RBCs among white cells.

Mean platelet volume (MPV) is a machine-calculated measurement of the average size of your platelets. New platelets are larger, and an increased MPV occurs when increased numbers of platelets are being produced. MPV gives your doctor information about platelet production in your bone marrow.
A measurement derived from the platelet count and the mean platelet volume.
Evalution of urine under the microscope, usually for cells, crystals, and/or infecting organisms.
Laboratory test for albumin in the urine in mass/volume units (mcg/ml, mg/L)
Derived from direct flow cytometric measurement of platelet cell volume, it is increased in the presence of platelet anisocytosis.

Fully specified name:en: Erythrocytes presence in urine sediment by light microscopy test
Description:en: A lab test to identify the presence of erythrocytes (red blood cells) in urine under a light microscope.

Fully specified name:en: Leukocytes presence in urine sediment by light microscopy
Description:en: A lab test to identify the presence of leukocytes in urine under a light microscope.
A lab test used to determine the presence of epithelial casts in urine specimen using a light microscope.

Fully specified name:en: Yeast presence in urine sediment by light microscopy
Description:en: A lab test to identify the presence of yeast cells in the urine using a light microscope.

Fully specified name:en: Yeast hyphae presence in urine sediment by light microscopy test
Description:en: A lab test to identify the presence of hyphae yeast in urine using a light microscope.
A lab test used to determine the presence of fungal spores in urine specimen using a light microscope.

Fully specified name:en: Trichomonas vaginalis presence in urine sediment by light microscopy
Description:en: A lab test used to determine the presence of trichomonas vaginalis in urine specimen using a light microscope.

Fully specified name:en: Casts presence in urine sediment by light microscopy test
Description:en: A lab test used to determine the presence of casts in urine specimen using a light microscope.

Fully specified name:en: Crystals type in urine sediment by light microscopy test
Description:en: A lab test used to determine the presence of crystals in urine specimen using a light microscope.
